intro: This example shows that raw Markdown syntax can still be captured as editable JSON sections.

sections.title: Inline HTML
sections.summary: Inline HTML can be preserved as raw Markdown text and edited through JSON.
sections.snippet:
Expand me

Hidden content can stay in the Markdown source.
notes: The raw block is preserved without inventing a new HTML model.
The section remains editable through JSON CRUD operations.

sections.title: Footnotes
sections.summary: Footnote references and definitions can be preserved as raw Markdown text.
sections.snippet:
Here is a sentence with a footnote.[^1]

[^1]: Footnotes are captured as raw markdown text.
notes: The source remains easy to edit.
The renderer does not need special footnote semantics to keep the text intact.

sections.title: Task Lists
sections.summary: Task list checkboxes can remain plain Markdown while still being tracked in JSON.
sections.snippet:
- [ ] Draft schema
- [x] Render output
- [ ] Verify round-trip
notes: Checkbox syntax stays visible in the output.
The JSON layer can still update the section body atomically.

sections.title: Nested Lists
sections.summary: Nested list syntax can be preserved as raw Markdown content.
notes: The structure remains readable in source control.
No custom nested-list model is required for pass-through use cases.

sections.title: Definition Lists
sections.summary: Definition list syntax can be carried through as raw text.
sections.snippet:
Term
: Definition
Another term
: Another definition
notes: The importer does not need to normalize this into a special shape.
Editors can still update the text through CRUD operations.

sections.title: Admonitions
sections.summary: Flavor-specific admonition syntax can live inside a raw section.
sections.snippet:
> [!NOTE]
> This content is preserved as blockquote-style markdown.
> It can still be edited as raw text.
notes: The fabric can preserve the syntax even when it does not interpret it semantically.
The source remains deterministic and diffable.

sections.title: HTML Tables
sections.summary: HTML table markup can be preserved as raw Markdown source when needed.
notes: This keeps custom table layouts intact.
It avoids forcing HTML into a simplified Markdown table model.

sections.title: Math
sections.summary: Inline and block math can remain raw source text in a Markdown section.
sections.snippet:
Euler said $e^{i\pi} + 1 = 0$ and display math can stay as plain text:
$$
x^2 + y^2 = z^2
$$
notes: Math delimiters stay visible in source.
A richer math renderer can be added later if we choose to invest in it.

sections.title: Embedded Media
sections.summary: Images, iframes, and other embedded media can be preserved as raw content.
notes: The section stays editable as raw Markdown text.
Media semantics can be layered on later if needed.

closing_note: The section body can remain raw text while still being fully editable through JSON CRUD operations.
